text.title: Team AI Usage Policy Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Policy says 'follow good judgment with AI'. Devs interpret as 'paste anything, ask if blocked'.
antipattern.root-cause: Policy authored by exec without operational input. Abstract guidance has no enforcement surface.
antipattern.fix: Replace principles with concrete lists. Each item is an action the dev does or does not do.

antipattern.symptom: Policy in a wiki page; no one knows who maintains it. 18 months later it's stale.
antipattern.root-cause: Policy committed without owner field. Quarterly review never happens.
antipattern.fix: Mandatory owner block (name + role + Slack). Quarterly review date in CI; stale policies auto-flag.

antipattern.symptom: 5-page policy with legal preamble. Read-completion rate < 15% per audit survey.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated policy as compliance artefact, not operational tool.
antipattern.fix: Cap at 1 page / 500 words. Move legal preamble to an appendix the dev does not need to read to act.

antipattern.symptom: Dev pastes customer PII; team scrambles for 4 hours without a runbook.
antipattern.root-cause: Policy listed prohibitions but no IR steps.
antipattern.fix: Add 4-step IR runbook with roles + time SLAs.
